  • Method Details

    • aipSin

      public AltInvestment.Builder aipSin(String aipSin)
      DTCC-assigned 16-character unique identifier for alternative investment products. Enables industry-wide standardization and DTCC AIP network integration.

    • aipSin

      public AltInvestment.Builder aipSin(Optional<String> aipSin)
      DTCC-assigned 16-character unique identifier for alternative investment products. Enables industry-wide standardization and DTCC AIP network integration.

    • estimatedValueDescriptor

      public AltInvestment.Builder estimatedValueDescriptor(EstimatedValueDescriptor estimatedValueDescriptor)
      Indicates whether the valuation represents estimate of investors' interest in the net assets of the program or estimate of the value of the security.

    • estimatedValueDescriptor

      public AltInvestment.Builder estimatedValueDescriptor(Optional<? extends EstimatedValueDescriptor> estimatedValueDescriptor)
      Indicates whether the valuation represents estimate of investors' interest in the net assets of the program or estimate of the value of the security.

    • ownershipStructure

      public AltInvestment.Builder ownershipStructure(OwnershipStructure ownershipStructure)
      Describes the legal entity structure of the alternative investment. Critical for tax treatment, regulatory compliance, and operational procedures.

    • ownershipStructure

      public AltInvestment.Builder ownershipStructure(Optional<? extends OwnershipStructure> ownershipStructure)
      Describes the legal entity structure of the alternative investment. Critical for tax treatment, regulatory compliance, and operational procedures.

    • taxReportingStructure

      public AltInvestment.Builder taxReportingStructure(AssetTaxReportingStructure taxReportingStructure)
      Indicates whether the investment generates IRS Form 1099 or Schedule K-1 for tax reporting. Critical for year-end tax processing.

    • taxReportingStructure

      public AltInvestment.Builder taxReportingStructure(Optional<? extends AssetTaxReportingStructure> taxReportingStructure)
      Indicates whether the investment generates IRS Form 1099 or Schedule K-1 for tax reporting. Critical for year-end tax processing.

    • unitizedCapitalIndicator

      public AltInvestment.Builder unitizedCapitalIndicator(boolean unitizedCapitalIndicator)
      Indicates whether the investment uses unitized accounting (shares/units with NAV), or capital account accounting (member capital accounts). Critical for transaction processing and position reporting. A value of true means Unitized. A value of false means Capital Account.

    • unitizedCapitalIndicator

      public AltInvestment.Builder unitizedCapitalIndicator(Optional<Boolean> unitizedCapitalIndicator)
      Indicates whether the investment uses unitized accounting (shares/units with NAV), or capital account accounting (member capital accounts). Critical for transaction processing and position reporting. A value of true means Unitized. A value of false means Capital Account.

    • universalFootnote

      public AltInvestment.Builder universalFootnote(UniversalFootnote universalFootnote)
      Universal footnote combining valuation descriptor, source, and method information.

    • universalFootnote

      public AltInvestment.Builder universalFootnote(Optional<? extends UniversalFootnote> universalFootnote)
      Universal footnote combining valuation descriptor, source, and method information.

    • valuationFrequency

      public AltInvestment.Builder valuationFrequency(ValuationFrequency valuationFrequency)
      Indicates how often the asset is valued. Critical for pricing workflows, client expectations, and operational scheduling.

    • valuationFrequency

      public AltInvestment.Builder valuationFrequency(Optional<? extends ValuationFrequency> valuationFrequency)
      Indicates how often the asset is valued. Critical for pricing workflows, client expectations, and operational scheduling.

    • valuationMethod

      public AltInvestment.Builder valuationMethod(ValuationMethod valuationMethod)
      Indicates the methodology used to determine the asset's value. Different valuation methods require different data sources and calculation workflows.

    • valuationMethod

      public AltInvestment.Builder valuationMethod(Optional<? extends ValuationMethod> valuationMethod)
      Indicates the methodology used to determine the asset's value. Different valuation methods require different data sources and calculation workflows.

    • valuationSource

      public AltInvestment.Builder valuationSource(ValuationSource valuationSource)
      Indicates whether the valuation was provided by management of program, third part firm conducting independent valuation or by broker/dealer.

    • valuationSource

      public AltInvestment.Builder valuationSource(Optional<? extends ValuationSource> valuationSource)
      Indicates whether the valuation was provided by management of program, third part firm conducting independent valuation or by broker/dealer.
